paragraphs.body: Dumplings are an important part of traditional Chinese food culture. They are not only delicious food, but also a symbol of reunion and happiness. Whether in traditional festivals such as the Spring Festival, or in daily life, dumplings are one of the common dishes of Chinese families.

Traditionally, dumplings are believed to have been invented by Zhang Zhongjing[4] who was a great practitioner of traditional Chinese medicine. During the era of the Eastern Han (AD 25–220)[2][3] by Jiaozi was originally called "Nen ear" (Chinese: Jiao ear; Because they're used to treat frostbitten ears. In winter, Zhang Zhongjing saw many people on his way home with frostbitten ears because they didn't have warm clothes or enough food. He stewed mutton, black pepper, and some warm medicine in a pot with these poor people, chopped them up, and filled them with little dough skins. He boiled these dumplings and gave them to his patients with the broth until the Chinese New Year. To celebrate the New Year and to recover from frostbitten ears, people imitate Zhang's recipe to make tender ears. Other theories suggest that jiaozi may have derived from dumplings in Western Asia. In the Western Han dynasty (206 BC – AD 9) jiaozi (餃子) were called jiaozi (角子). During the Three Kingdoms period (AD 220–280), the book Guangya by Zhang Yi mentions jiaozi.

paragraphs.body: Dumplings have a rich symbolic meaning in Chinese culture. First of all, the shape of the dumpling is similar to the ancient gold ingots, symbolizing wealth and prosperity. Therefore, Jiaozi is one of the major dishes eaten during the Chinese New Year throughout northern China and eaten all year round in the northern provinces.. In addition, dumplings are usually made when family members gather together, so it also represents reunion and family affection. The whole family sits together to make dumplings, symbolizing family happiness. In addition, with the process of globalization, dumplings are becoming popular around the world, especially in Asian neighbors, Japan, South Korea and other places. There are similar foods, such as Japanese dumplings "dumplings" and South Korea's "dumplings".

paragraphs.body: Gyōza and gyōza wrappers can be found in markets and restaurants throughout Japan, either frozen or ready to eat. Pan-fried gyōza are sold as a side dish in many ramen and Chinese restaurants. Both the wrappers and the prepared gyōza themselves are increasingly easy to find in Asian markets around the world. The most popular preparation method is the pan-fried style called yaki-gyōza (焼き餃子), in which the dumpling is first fried on one flat side, creating a crispy skin. Then, water is added and the pan sealed with a lid, until the upper part of the dumpling is steamed. This technique is what the Chinese call making potstickers (see above). Other popular methods include boiling sui-gyōza (水餃子) and deep frying age-gyōza (揚げ餃子 ).

paragraphs.body: In the West, dumplings are also becoming more and more popular. Many Chinese restaurants have introduced dumplings as an important dish, and different flavors of dumplings have attracted more and more foreign people. For example, Boiled dumplings, Steamed dumplings, Pan-fried dumplings. Deep-fried dumplings and Soup dumplings. The diversification of modern society makes the production of dumplings no longer adhere to the traditional way, a variety of innovative flavors of dumplings emerge endlessly, such as vegetarian dumplings, seafood dumplings, etc., to meet the needs of different tastes.

In conclusion, As a traditional Chinese food, dumplings carry profound cultural connotations. From the origin to the evolution of modern times, dumplings are not only a taste of enjoyment, but also an important symbol of cultural inheritance and family ties. Whether in Chinese festival celebrations or on modern international tables, dumplings continue to win people's affection with their unique charm and rich meaning. In today's globalization, dumplings have crossed regional boundaries and become a part of the world's food culture, symbolizing people's common pursuit of a better life.
